The college is committed to continuously upgrading its facilities to provide quality technical education that meets both industrial and societal needs. [Entity: MSAJCE | Category: admission | Source: admission.txt] # Admission Information for B.E/B.Tech, M.E/M.Tech, and Ph.D Programs

This document outlines the admission process, eligibility criteria, and available programs for undergraduate, postgraduate, and research courses at MSAJCE.

## Undergraduate Programmes
The college offers a variety of undergraduate programs, each with a total sanctioned intake divided into government and management quotas. The programs include:

1. Civil Engineering with an intake of 30, split evenly between the government and management quotas.
2. Computer Science & Engineering with a total intake of 60, again evenly split.

[Entity: MSAJCE | Category: admission | Source: admission.txt] management quotas.
2. Computer Science & Engineering with a total intake of 60, again evenly split.
3. Electronics & Communication Engineering and Electrical & Electronics Engineering both have an intake of 60 and 30, respectively.
4. Mechanical Engineering has an intake of 30, and a similar structure is followed for Information Technology and specialized programs like Artificial Intelligence & Data Science, Computer Science & Business Systems, and others.

For students outside Tamil Nadu, admissions are based on qualifying marks in Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry. 

## Postgraduate Progr

[Entity: MSAJCE | Category: admission | Source: admission.txt] ssions are based on qualifying marks in Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry. 

## Postgraduate Programmes
The postgraduate offerings include:

1. M.E. in Computer Science and Engineering, requiring a foundational degree in related fields such as EEE, ECE, CSE, or IT.
2. M.E. in Structural Engineering requires a B.E. in Civil Engineering.

Eligibility criteria follow admission regulations based on candidates' performance in TANCET, operated by Anna University.

The institution offers a Ph.D. program in Mechanical Engineering. Admission requires a Master's degree or

[Entity: MSAJCE | Category: admission | Source: admission.txt] stitution offers a Ph.D. program in Mechanical Engineering. Admission requires a Master's degree or equivalent in the relevant field. Additional qualifications include substantial research experience and publications in reputed journals.

Two models of skill development courses are available, focusing on job rolls in Computer Science and Mechanical Engineering. Programs prepare students for roles such as Computer Hardware Network Maintenance and Refrigeration & Air Conditioning Technician.
